# **President Trump lands in Vietnam for second historic summit with North Korea**

**Air Force One** touched down in Hanoi, Vietnam, at 8:54 a.m. ET this morning as President Donald J. Trump prepares for his second summit with Chairman Kim Jong Un of North Korea this week.

**“As part of a bold new diplomacy** , we continue our historic push for peace on the Korean Peninsula,” President Trump said in this month’s State of the Union address.

**Last June’s meeting** in Singapore marked the first time in history that an American president has met with the leader of North Korea. This week’s summit looks to build on the progress made that day, including transformed relations between the two countries and the complete denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula—a goal that seemed virtually unachievable for decades.

**America’s negotiations** with Chairman Kim are already producing results, both for our citizens and for our allies:
* North Korea has not conducted a nuclear weapons or missile test in more than 400 days.
* The regime has committed to dismantling its plutonium and uranium enrichment facilities.
* American citizens are no longer detained in North Korea.
* North Korea has returned remains of Americans who died during the Korean War—and President Trump is working to return more.

**President Trump’s strategy** is working where past administrations have failed for one reason above all else: America wins when it negotiates from a position of strength. Upon taking office, President Trump mobilized a global coalition to enforce a maximum pressure campaign on the regime in Pyongyang. He made clear that the world would not accept a nuclear-armed North Korea—and that provocations would come with a cost.

**Now, instead of hopelessness** , there is opportunity. The President has made clear that should Chairman Kim follow through on his promise to denuclearize, the Trump Administration will work to ensure there are economic development options for North Korea. The United States and its partners are prepared to explore how to mobilize investment, improve infrastructure, enhance food security, and more.

**“Our hostages have come home** , nuclear testing has stopped, and there has not been a missile launch in more than 15 months,” President Trump told Congress three weeks ago. There is still work to be done, but the early returns are clear: A strong United States of America makes for a stronger, safer world.

[**Tod Lindberg: Why the Trump approach beats the Obama one**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6f6578054b&e=3ee4985eea)

**The facts:** **[President Trump’s historic push for peace on the Korean Peninsula](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b851631a33&e=3ee4985eea)**

Peter -- when we launched this campaign, we knew we'd only be successful if we built it person by person, one donation at a time. So my team and I just took a look to see where we are for February, and the truth is we're falling behind our goals for our movement by 3,417 donors.
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976a73/2ae112ef/107671fd/357af9e4/56814492/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI3NjMtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2763-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
It's critical we build a campaign that lifts your voices over the influence of the special interests who've corrupted our politics. A community-led movement is the only way to secure a Green New Deal, Medicare for All and an end to the NRA's stronghold on the national stage.
That's why this campaign won't take a dime from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists, and we won't have an individual super PAC. It's the grassroots against dark money--and while they can write big checks, we have the power of our voices. **[But right now, we still need 3,417 more donors before the end of the month to meet our goal. I'm counting on you to make a contribution before midnight Thursday to keep growing our networks, building critical infrastructure in the early states and reaching more voters.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976a73/2ae112ef/107671fd/357af9e4/56814492/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI3NjMtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2763-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
**Here 's the link to donate--whatever amount you can makes a difference: [https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976a73/2ae112ef/107671fd/357af9e4/56814492/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI3NjMtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2763-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
There's so much corruption and greed built up in Washington. Special interests have accumulated power and influence, and they won't let it go without a fight. But if we build a true, grassroots movement, we can restore power to the heart of our communities that takes on:
* insurance companies--and achieves Medicare for All
* the big banks that gouge students on loans--and instead, moves to debt-free college
* Big Oil, Big Pharma and the NRA: If they aren't in it for you, we will take them on.

**But we can 't do any of this unless we meet our February goal--so we're counting on you to be one of the new donors we need before Thursday. We have to stay on track to prove that our community-led, grassroots campaign can take on corporate greed and _win_. [Please, give anything you can right now. Any amount gets us closer to our month-end goal.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976a73/2ae112ef/107671fd/357af9e4/56814492/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azI3NjMtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk2763-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

# **MESSAGE FROM MARY**
Dear Peter, Change sometimes makes people cling more tightly to that which seems safe and familiar. As the demographics in Tennessee change and we become a more diverse and inclusive state, there is only one way forward: at **_every level of government_**** ** we must introduce a BOLD, economic agenda that works for EVERY Tennessean, i.e. invest in ALL people - **_no matter who you are, what you look like, or where you live - _**rebuild our infrastructure, and prioritize budgets as moral documents. The hard work we do today to fight for the future of our people, our economy, the soul of our country, and our planet is the most important work we can do. This is not a moment, it's a movement and we need __**every Tennessee Democrat**__ to step up and help make it happen. Here's what you can do: **RUN FOR SOMETHING** Across the country, people who don't look like traditional politicians are running for office - and winning. You are no different. Tennessee is no different. You know your neighbors, friends and family and the problems that exist in your own community. Find the problems you are most passionate about solving, figure out which legislative body that addresses these issues, and, in the words of Democratic Party organizer Amanda Litman, _**Run for Something!**_ Email [evan@tndp.org](https://act.myngp.com/el/NkexuY0UWDVaLD8ngnEz4AHt9DZeesTMluyj6dBcrcM=/A3Av2flqyi4P673FuAEjyAi5IhXIx0FObbmgeHHutz0=?subject=I%20Want%20to%20Run%20for%20Something) for more info. Our government, at every level, has the responsibility to fix the serious economic problems which have held back so many Tennesseans and their families. Be a part of the change you want to see in the state. **LEAD YOUR LOCAL DEMOCRATIC PARTY** We are led from the ground up at the county level and together we work to elect Democrats who will introduce bold ideas and policy to help every Tennessean fulfill their hopes, dreams, aspirations and promise. During the last week in March and the first week of April, every Tennessee county will hold a LEAD 2020 event in which they - YOU - will elect new leadership. If you share our deep concern for the well-being of your family, your friends, your neighbors, your community, and your country, I encourage you to join the Tennessee Democratic Party as we work at the grassroots level to elect Democrats and reclaim our state for the sake of all of us. Your leadership is what the Tennessee Democratic Party needs. Learn more below and at [tndp.org/lead2020](https://act.myngp.com/el/NkexuY0UWDVaLD8ngnEz4AHt9DZeesTMluyj6dBcrcM=/5TVB3l94Bb_bUfvx3eQRPli112AFOm2TuTI8BzPvFQo=). **HELP US AMPLIFY OUR MESSAGE** We can always use the help of dedicated Democrats like you to help us amplify our message. Peter,

Today, Rep. Pramila Jayapal will introduce the Medicare For All Act of 2019. I am supporting this legislation because this crisis we are facing requires bold action. We must implement a single public program that gives comprehensive coverage to all — no exceptions. When we create a system that works for all, we will see health outcomes improve and costs go down.

This is a critical moment in history. 70 percent of Americans support Medicare for All. Transitioning to Medicare for All will save us trillions of dollars that are wasted on clipboards and profits. We cannot squander this opportunity. We must take bold action, as representatives and activists, constituents and voters, to dramatically strengthen the American safety net.

[**Donate to our campaign and I will fight to pass Medicare for All. We know that the Texas 10th is a battleground district, as are several other nearby Congressional seats, which gives us an opportunity to lead. To set the standard for what a progressive campaign looks like. To fight without equivocation for the needs of the greatest number of Americans.Here in Texas we know the stakes. A sky-high maternal mortality rate. Hospital closures in rural counties, that force seniors and mothers to drive hours to seek adequate care. Sick people in bankruptcy because they cannot afford their doctor bills. Retired teachers choosing between paying their prescriptions and paying their electricity.
[**That is what I will do in Congress — fight without equivocation. I will fight for what I believe in. Will you join me in that fight by supporting our race?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/5076399/42994983/-917578712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTEyY2JkNS1iMTNhLWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KAlDeN6MztedVPw_q3HKBxZkiJNni5_HOtPUxiAF7iA=&refcode=medicareem&emci=0912cbd5-b13a-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&emdi=0a12cbd5-b13a-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&ceid=6233172)
We cannot be deterred by the hospital lobby, the pharmaceutical lobby, the HMO lobby. The transition to universal healthcare may be difficult for some. And we must ensure that we are giving healthcare workers who are impacted by this transition a just transition, to make sure they can support their families and contribute to society. But we cannot let these obstacles stand in the way of healthcare for all.

I will fight for Medicare for All in Congress.

Mike Siegel

body:
There have been a few important new updates on Abigail Spanberger's first re-election campaign for Congress over the last few weeks, so we want to quickly make sure you're up to date:
| | Hi Peter,

There have been a few important new updates on Abigail Spanberger's first re-election campaign for Congress over the last few weeks, so we want to quickly make sure you're up to date:

**This month, the National Republican Campaign Committee put Abigail at the top of their target list for 2020.** And as a reminder: The final vote tally in this ultra-competitive Virginia district in 2018 was 50.4% to 48.4% -- Abigail won by very slim margin that was powered by grassroots volunteers and supporters. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/0N7O4oNnbqqWCnR9rlA4Kg3eSKKm-xHee2BBk2UNgPQ=/iq4hVCdHJKsAgTAv7kDo-SZJrj23ON3uCn_olaQ80jE=?refcode=em_fr_2019.02.27_b1_tm_all&amount=25) And on the very same day that the National Republican Campaign Committee announced they were going to target this district again, the Chairwoman of the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, Rep. Cheri Bustos from Illinois, added Abigail to the "Frontline" Program. The Frontline Program is reserved for only the most competitive elections for House Democrats.

But, there's one more very important fact that we need to make sure is on your radar, Peter: **Our end-of-month fundraising deadline is just 24 hours away.**

And, since we know that Washington Republicans (and special interest groups) are targeting this seat, we really need to make sure we hit our $7,000 weekly fundraising goal to close out the month.

It's clear that this next election is going to be competitive and expensive for our campaign. Abigail Spanberger is depending on grassroots support from folks like you. We know that people-powered campaigns are the ones that make the most impact, so that's why we're asking for your help. [**Will you donate to help us hit this important February deadline goal? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/0N7O4oNnbqqWCnR9rlA4Kg3eSKKm-xHee2BBk2UNgPQ=/iq4hVCdHJKsAgTAv7kDo-SZJrj23ON3uCn_olaQ80jE=?refcode=em_fr_2019.02.27_b1_tm_all&amount=25)

Thanks for all you do,

_Team Spanberger_
| [CONTRIBUTE](https://act.myngp.com/el/0N7O4oNnbqqWCnR9rlA4Kg3eSKKm-xHee2BBk2UNgPQ=/iq4hVCdHJKsAgTAv7kDo-SZJrj23ON3uCn_olaQ80jE=?refcode=em_fr_2019.02.27_b1_tm_all&amount=25)

subject: President Trump Praises ‘Special Relationship’ with North Korea’s Kim Jong Un at Start of Hanoi Summit
body:
"President Trump praised his ‘special relationship’ with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and said ‘a lot of things will be solved’." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ec1aac3951&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Praises ‘Special Relationship’ with North Korea’s Kim Jong Un at Start of Hanoi Summit**

“President Trump praised his ‘special relationship’ with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and said ‘a lot of things will be solved’ during the two leaders' second summit in Vietnam as they prepare for a full day of formal talks,” Fox News reports.

“Trump and Kim kicked off their second summit on Wednesday by exchanging handshakes and laughs at the Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi. They briefly spoke to reporters, where Trump said the biggest progress since the historic Singapore summit was the two countries' relationship.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b52d702467&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Yesterday, Fiat Chrysler announced that Detroit, Michigan, “will get a Jeep factory, the Motor City’s first new auto assembly plant in a generation, as part of a $4.5 billion manufacturing expansion that will mean nearly 6,500 new jobs,” Amy Huschka reports for the _[Detroit Free Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=354fc11d74&e=3ee4985eea)_. With jobs coming back to the upper Midwest thanks to the promise of the new U.S.–Mexico–Canada Agreement, President Trump praised the move: “Thank you Fiat Chrysler. They are all coming back to the USA, it’s where the action is!”
---
|
---
| “A previously deported illegal alien from Honduras, Juan Ramon-Vasquez, has been sentenced in Philadelphia after being convicted of repeatedly raping a child. Philadelphia is a sanctuary city and he will serve just 21 months in jail,” Katie Pavlich writes in _[Townhall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d467019dd9&e=3ee4985eea)_. In March 2014, Ramon-Vasquez was found back in the U.S. by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers, but the City of Philadelphia ignored an ICE detainer and released him. Upon release, the criminal alien “proceeded to repeatedly rape a young child,” the Department of Justice said.
---
|
---
| “An abortion survivor is slamming Senate Democrats for blocking a bill that would have protected babies born alive in failed abortions. The Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act on Monday was voted down 53-44, with only Democrats voting against it,” [Fox News Insider](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c1cbcd8e62&e=3ee4985eea) reports. “They're willing to sacrifice lives like mine,” Melissa Ohden said yesterday. Ms. Ohden, who survived a failed abortion in 1977, said that despite what Democrats say, legislation like the Born-Alive law is necessary—and she's living proof.

Are you watching Michael Cohen testify before Congress today?
